title: Top 10 Games I'm Looking Forward to in 2019
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=D50nmITTLp8

Daniel (@dungeondive) dives into his most anticipated board games for 2019, and spoiler alert—most are Kickstarter projects. The list kicks off with Secrets of the Lost Station, the sci-fi sequel to his all-time favorite, though Daniel admits the sterile spaceship setting makes him slightly less excited than he'd like to be. Other standouts include the ambitious Dungeon Universalis (the "it'll be a miracle if this comes out in 2019" pick), the heavily narrative Tainted Grail, and the meta-gaming delight of D6 Dungeons. James M. Hewitt's Hellboy takes the mid-list slot with fantastic IP and cool miniatures, while Machina Arcana 2nd Edition promises a streamlined take on steampunk Lovecraftian exploration. The top three—Skull Tales with its Pirates of the Caribbean meets horror vibe, the massively ambitious Madeira, and Roger Deering's passion project Dungeon Crusade—represent the kind of massive, detailed dungeon experiences Daniel lives for, complete with sprawling maps, hundreds of quests, and enough components to fill multiple tables.

What catches Daniel's enthusiasm across these games is the promise of rich non-combat encounters, deep exploration mechanics, and the kind of design ambition that makes him lose sleep. Of course, being Kickstarter projects, there's always that asterisk: will they actually ship? Will they live up to the hype? That's part of the adventure.

----

Which of these 2019 games ended up delivering on Daniel's high expectations, and more importantly—are there any of his anticipated picks that completely surprised him (for better or worse) once they actually hit the table?
